# UC-22.11.44 · Privilege Escalation Chains on CDE Windows Servers

## Description

Correlates token elevation, service installation, and logon type sequences on payment servers so unauthorized privilege escalation is detected and investigated under access restriction obligations.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes windows and sourcetypes WinEventLog:Security. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Tune `maxspan` to environment. (2) Add known patch service names to allow list. (3) Create ES correlation search from this pattern. (4) Retain transactions in summary index with redaction. Indexes required: `index=windows`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security`; Lookups: `pci_payment_servers`, `pci_payment_servers.csv`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=windows` `WinEventLog:Security` EventCode 4672,4698,7045; `lookup pci_payment_servers.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Tune `maxspan` to environment. (2) Add known patch service names to allow list. (3) Create ES correlation search from this pattern. (4) Retain transactions in summary index with redaction.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode IN (4672,4698,7045) earliest=-24h
| lookup pci_payment_servers.csv ComputerName OUTPUT pci_host
| where pci_host="true"
| transaction ComputerName maxspan=300m maxevents=20 startswith=EventCode=4672 endswith=EventCode=7045
| where eventcount>=3
| table duration, ComputerName, EventCode, Security_ID, Account_Name
```

Understanding this SPL

**Privilege Escalation Chains on CDE Windows Servers (PCI DSS Req 7.2.5, 10.2.2)** — Correlates token elevation, service installation, and logon type sequences on payment servers so unauthorized privilege escalation is detected and investigated under access restriction obligations.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=windows` `WinEventLog:Security` EventCode 4672,4698,7045; `lookup pci_payment_servers.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: windows; **sourcetype**: WinEventLog:Security. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=windows, sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where pci_host="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Groups related events into transactions — prefer `maxspan`/`maxpause`/`maxevents` for bounded memory.
• Filters the current rows with `where eventcount>=3` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Privilege Escalation Chains on CDE Windows Servers (PCI DSS Req 7.2.5, 10.2.2)** — Correlates token elevation, service installation, and logon type sequences on payment servers so unauthorized privilege escalation is detected and investigated under access restriction obligations.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=windows` `WinEventLog:Security` EventCode 4672,4698,7045; `lookup pci_payment_servers.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: Account_Name, ComputerName, EventCode, Security_ID, duration.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (transaction), table (suspicious hosts), single value (transaction count).

## SPL

```spl
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode IN (4672,4698,7045) earliest=-24h
| lookup pci_payment_servers.csv ComputerName OUTPUT pci_host
| where pci_host="true"
| transaction ComputerName maxspan=300m maxevents=20 startswith=EventCode=4672 endswith=EventCode=7045
| where eventcount>=3
| table duration, ComputerName, EventCode, Security_ID, Account_Name
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Timeline (transaction), table (suspicious hosts), single value (transaction count). Key fields: `Account_Name`, `ComputerName`, `EventCode`, `Security_ID`, `duration`.
